identificationModule.officialTitle: A Comparison of Liposomal Bupivacaine Versus Ropivacaine for Interscalene Brachial Plexus Block in Alleviating Rebound Pain Following Shoulder Arthroscopy

interventions.name: Liposomal Bupivicaine
interventions.description: patients randomized to the intervention arm received 133mg of liposomal bupivacaine

interventions.name: ropivacaine
interventions.description: those in the control group received 80mg of ropivacaine

primaryOutcomes.measure: incidence of rebound pain in two groups.
primaryOutcomes.description: Rebound pain (RP) was defined as the abrupt transition from well-controlled pain (Numerical Rating Scale, NRS ≤ 3) during the effective period of the block to severe pain (NRS ≥ 7) within 24 hours of block performance

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* adults between 18 and 65 year-old
* American Society of Anesthesiologist (ASA) grades 1 to 3 physical classification status
* patients unilateral primary arthroscopic shoulder surgery lasting 1to 4 hours

Exclusion Criteria:

* had an allergy or intolerance to amide-type local anesthetics
* objective evidence of nerve damage in the affected upper limb
* local infection
* impaired renal function (defined as effective glomerular filtration rate \< 30ml/min/1.73m2)
* coagulation disorders
* respiratory compromise（requires long term oxygen）
* had participated in another research trial involving an investigational medicinal product in the 6 months before randomization
